skill.name: claude-scientific-skills
skill.description: Provides concise guidance for scientific research planning, literature review, analysis framing, and reporting.
skill.summary: Guides scientific research and analysis workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported hardcoded URLs and weak cryptographic algorithm patterns in SKILL.md. Review found the URLs are source repository references and the crypto hits are false positives in ordinary metadata and prose, with no executable code, network calls, prompt injection, or secret handling found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL References Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. These locations contain source repository references only; there is no code that performs a network request or sends data to the URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are static source attribution links in Markdown and frontmatter. No executable network API, script, or data transfer path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match Is a Text False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. The file contains only descriptive text about scientific research skills and does not implement or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only available file is a 23-line Markdown instruction file. The flagged locations are metadata and prose, not cryptographic code or security guidance.

content.value_statement: Scientific work often needs clear structure before analysis begins. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code frame research tasks, organize evidence, and plan analysis outputs.
actual_capabilities: Provides general guidance for scientific research workflows.
Helps frame research questions and analysis goals.
Supports planning for literature review and evidence organization.
Guides structure for scientific summaries and reports.
Works as a lightweight instruction skill for Claude-compatible tools.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, datasets, or analysis notebooks.
Does not fetch papers, verify citations, or access external databases.
Does not replace domain expert review or statistical validation.
Provides broad guidance rather than field-specific protocols.

use_cases.title: Plan a Research Review
use_cases.description: Create a clear outline for reviewing papers, extracting evidence, and summarizing findings.

use_cases.title: Frame an Analysis Task
use_cases.description: Turn a broad scientific question into variables, assumptions, methods, and expected outputs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Scientific Notes
use_cases.description: Organize observations, caveats, and conclusions into a concise research summary.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Research Question
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me turn this topic into a focused scientific research question: [topic]. Include scope, key variables, and likely evidence sources.

prompt_templates.title: Structure a Literature Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a literature review plan for [research area]. Include search themes, inclusion criteria, comparison points, and a summary structure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Analysis Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an analysis workflow for [dataset or experiment]. Include assumptions, methods, quality checks, limitations, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Critique a Research Argument
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this research argument for clarity, evidence quality, assumptions, alternative explanations, and missing validation steps: [argument].

output_examples.input: Plan a review of studies about battery degradation in cold climates.
output: A focused research question about temperature effects on battery capacity retention.
A review structure covering chemistry, test conditions, degradation mechanisms, and measurement methods.
A list of caveats about study comparability and operational conditions.

output_examples.input: Help organize an analysis of plant growth under different light levels.
output: A proposed analysis plan with variables, controls, and outcome measures.
Quality checks for sample size, measurement consistency, and confounding factors.
A reporting outline for methods, results, limitations, and conclusions.

output_examples.input: Critique my draft conclusion for a clinical pilot study.
output: A concise assessment of whether claims match the evidence.
Suggested language for uncertainty, sample limitations, and future validation.
Questions to address before sharing the conclusion with reviewers.

best_practices: Provide the research question, domain, data type, and intended audience.
Ask for assumptions, limitations, and validation checks in every analysis plan.
Use the output as a planning aid and verify technical claims with trusted sources.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to invent citations or claim access to papers it has not seen.
Do not treat broad research guidance as statistical proof or peer review.
Do not omit domain context when asking for methods or interpretation.

faq.question: Does this skill run scientific analysis code?
faq.answer: No. It provides research and analysis guidance, but it does not include scripts or notebooks.

faq.question: Can it find and download papers?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not include network tools or database access for literature retrieval.

faq.question: Is it useful for literature reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help structure review questions, comparison criteria, and summary formats.

faq.question: Can it replace a subject matter expert?
faq.answer: No. Scientific claims, methods, and conclusions still need expert validation.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the research topic, data context, audience, constraints, and the output format you need.
